mentions.note: Dollar stores and discounters experiencing euphoric retail jailbreak on lower inflation; buyers can't find exit.

CEO Todd Boasso turnaround impressive—store standards, supply chain, labor efficiency all fixed. Cramer visited store 611 in person; operational improvements confirmed aisle-by-aisle. Stock up 98% YTD.
Value-oriented retailer; makes things, sells cheaper; understable business model; reports tomorrow.
Bargain offering, real value; unless oil hits $120, fits description of what's working; will go higher.
Trade-down play; reports tomorrow (Mar 11). Lower price point attracts economically stressed consumers in oil-driven recession. Buy on pullback.
Off game currently, but historically resilient—don't bet against; likely to pull bull out of hat as it usually does.
Caller Robert bullish; new CEO (Jerry J. Fleman, ex-grocery exec) expanding food offerings, 450 new US stores + 10 Mexico planned. Right environment for discount retail. Good value story.
Down 2.6%; should benefit when economy slows, but weakness suggests consumer hurt more than expected.
4.3% same-store sales growth; value retailer performing better than OLLI's 3.6% comp.
Worst S&P performer today but should be bought in downturns not sold; war-driven panic overdone; traditional recession proxy.
Expected to report Tuesday following Dollar Tree's success; value retailer likely to show continued resilience in consumer spending.

Core customers financially constrained; fuel costs rising, SNAP benefits reduced, trip consolidation lowering shopping frequency. Thesis for weak-economy play has reversed; no longer benefiting from economic stress.
